config.name: Corporate — UBO / Ownership
config.tagline: Unwind circular ownership to the true beneficial owner — pure OAG.
config.use_case: Regulators require knowing the real human Ultimate Beneficial Owner (UBO) behind a corporate customer, but ownership is deliberately obscured through layered holdings across jurisdictions. The engine traverses the company-owns-company graph through multiple layers to compute effective control and resolve the true UBO — here the signal is the shape of the ownership graph itself.
config.what_you_see: Click an alert to investigate. Watch the engine unwind a loop of companies that own each other (hiding the owner) and a cluster sharing one UBO, then propose escalating to Enhanced Due Diligence (KYC review).

signals.label: Circular ownership
signals.why: Holding companies own each other in a loop, obscuring the UBO.

signals.label: Shared-UBO cluster
signals.why: Many companies declare one common ultimate beneficial owner.

signals.detail: Closed loop detected among these parties (A->B->C->A) with little economic substance.
5 ostensibly unrelated parties link to one UBO (UBO-01) — linked-identity ring.

CO-A.narrative: A suspicious circular ownership pattern has been detected in the Corporate - UBO/Ownership sector, typified as circular_ownership. Specifically, object_id CO-A is linked to object_id CO-B, which in turn is linked to object_id CO-C, which closes the loop back to CO-A (CO-A -> CO-B -> CO-C -> CO-A), indicating a circular structure with seemingly little economic substance. Furthermore, five ostensibly unrelated parties associated with object_ids CO-SHELL-00 and CO-SHELL-01 are all linked to UBO-01. This linkage to one identified Ultimate Beneficial Owner, UBO-01, is indicative of a potential "linked-identity ring" scenario. The presence of this circular ownership structure (CO-A) warrants escalation due to its high potential for indicating illicit or clandestine business arrangements. Therefore, I propose escalating the alert to a case for further investigation.
